source_paper_title: Upper Bounds for All and Max-Gain Policy Iteration Algorithms on Deterministic MDPs
context:
Section 6 (Summary and outlook), page 31

It remains unknown if such a Hamiltonian path through the set of policies exists for any MDP with \(k \ge 3\) actions. A concrete first step in pursuit of a “Hamiltonian” lower bound could be to attempt constructing a 3-state, 3-action MDP in which all 27 policies can be visited by PI.
title: Hamiltonian policy-improvement path for some MDP with \(k\ge 3\) actions
latex:
Given integers \(n\ge 3\) and \(k\ge 3\), let \(M=(S,A,T,R,\gamma)\) be a finite discounted Markov decision process (MDP), where \(S\) is a finite set of states with \(|S|=n\), \(A\) is a finite set of actions with \(|A|=k\), \(T:S\times A\times S\to[0,1]\) is a transition function satisfying \(\sum_{s'\in S}T(s,a,s')=1\) for all \((s,a)\in S\times A\), \(R:S\times A\to\mathbb{R}\) is a reward function, and \(\gamma\in[0,1)\) is a discount factor.

A (deterministic, stationary) policy is a function \(\pi:S\to A\). Let \(\Pi\) denote the set of all policies; then \(|\Pi|=k^n\). For a policy \(\pi\in\Pi\), define its value function \(V^{\pi}:S\to\mathbb{R}\) by
\[
V^{\pi}(s)=\mathbb{E}\Big[\sum_{t=0}^{\infty}\gamma^t R(s_t,\pi(s_t))\,\Big|\,s_0=s,\ s_{t+1}\sim T(s_t,\pi(s_t),\cdot)\Big].
\]
For \(s\in S\) and \(a\in A\), define the action-value \(Q^{\pi}(s,a)=R(s,a)+\gamma\sum_{s'\in S}T(s,a,s')V^{\pi}(s')\) and the gain \(\rho^{\pi}(s,a)=Q^{\pi}(s,a)-V^{\pi}(s)\).

Say that \(\pi'\in\Pi\) is obtainable from \(\pi\in\Pi\) by one policy-improvement step if for every \(s\in S\), either \(\pi'(s)=\pi(s)\) or \(\rho^{\pi}(s,\pi'(s))>0\), and there exists at least one \(s\) with \(\pi'(s)\ne\pi(s)\). Define the policy-improvement directed acyclic graph (PI-DAG) of \(M\) as the digraph with vertex set \(\Pi\) and an arc \(\pi\to\pi'\) whenever \(\pi'\) is obtainable from \(\pi\) by one policy-improvement step.

The problem is to determine whether there exists any discounted MDP \(M\) with \(|S|=n\) and \(|A|=k\) (for some \(n\ge 3\), \(k\ge 3\)) whose PI-DAG contains a Hamiltonian directed path, i.e., a directed path that visits all \(k^n\) policies exactly once. In particular, decide whether there exists a \(3\)-state, \(3\)-action discounted MDP \(M\) whose PI-DAG contains a directed path visiting all \(3^3=27\) policies.
open_problem_quote: It remains unknown if such a Hamiltonian path through the set of policies exists for any MDP with \(k \ge 3\) actions. A concrete first step in pursuit of a “Hamiltonian” lower bound could be to attempt constructing a 3-state, 3-action MDP in which all 27 policies can be visited by PI.
subject_classification: Markov Decision Processes; Discrete Optimization / Graph Algorithms
